subject: general-knowledge
description: International Airlines MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Ural Airlines is a major regional carrier based in which country?
mcqs.explanation: Ural Airlines is a Russian airline based in Yekaterinburg — one of the largest regional carriers in Russia.

mcqs.q: What is the flag carrier of Afghanistan, founded in 1955?
mcqs.explanation: Ariana Afghan Airlines, founded in 1955, is the flag carrier of Afghanistan and its oldest airline.

mcqs.q: Which airline is the national carrier of Bangladesh, founded in 1972?
mcqs.explanation: Biman Bangladesh Airlines, founded in 1972 just after independence, is the national flag carrier of Bangladesh.

mcqs.q: Sun Country Airlines is a low-cost leisure carrier from which country?
mcqs.explanation: Sun Country Airlines is a US-based low-cost leisure carrier headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota.

mcqs.q: Which is Canada's second-largest airline known for its low-cost model?
mcqs.explanation: WestJet, headquartered in Calgary, is Canada's second-largest airline and is known for its low-cost model.

mcqs.q: Continental Airlines merged with which major carrier in 2012?
mcqs.explanation: Continental Airlines merged with United Airlines in 2012, creating one of the world's largest carriers.

mcqs.q: Virgin Atlantic was founded by which entrepreneur in 1984?
mcqs.explanation: Virgin Atlantic was founded by British entrepreneur Richard Branson in 1984 as part of his Virgin Group.

mcqs.q: What is Japan's largest private airline known for service excellence?
mcqs.explanation: All Nippon Airways (ANA), founded in 1952, is Japan's largest private airline and is renowned for its service excellence — a 5-star Skytrax airline.

mcqs.q: China Airlines is the flag carrier of which territory?
mcqs.explanation: China Airlines is the flag carrier of Taiwan (Republic of China), not mainland China. Air China is the PRC's flag carrier.

mcqs.q: What is the official national airline of the People's Republic of China?
mcqs.explanation: Air China is the official flag carrier of the People's Republic of China, headquartered in Beijing.

mcqs.q: Which Israeli airline is considered the world's most secure?
mcqs.explanation: El Al Israel Airlines is the flag carrier of Israel and is considered the world's most secure airline — with armed sky marshals on every flight and bullet-proof cockpit doors.

mcqs.q: easyJet is a leading low-cost carrier from which country?
mcqs.explanation: easyJet is a British low-cost airline headquartered at London Luton Airport.

mcqs.q: Delta Air Lines is a major airline based in which US city?
mcqs.explanation: Delta Air Lines is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia — its primary hub is Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.

mcqs.q: EVA Air is a highly ranked airline from which country?
mcqs.explanation: EVA Air is a Taiwanese airline founded in 1989, consistently ranked among the world's best by Skytrax.

mcqs.q: Which is the world's oldest airline still operating under its original name?
mcqs.explanation: KLM Royal Dutch Airlines was founded in 1919 and is the world's oldest airline still operating under its original name.

mcqs.q: Which airline claims to be the oldest continuously operating airline?
mcqs.explanation: Qantas of Australia, founded in 1920, claims to be the world's oldest continuously operating airline.

mcqs.q: Olympic Air is a carrier based in which country?
mcqs.explanation: Olympic Air is a Greek airline, the successor of the historic Olympic Airways (founded by Aristotle Onassis in 1957).

mcqs.q: DELAG, the world's first airline (1909), was from which country?
mcqs.explanation: DELAG (Deutsche Luftschiffahrts-Aktiengesellschaft) was founded in Germany in 1909 to operate Zeppelin airships — the world's first commercial airline.

mcqs.q: American Airlines is the largest airline in the world by which metric?
mcqs.explanation: American Airlines is the world's largest airline by fleet size and passengers carried — based in Fort Worth, Texas.

mcqs.q: Middle East Airlines (MEA) is the national carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Middle East Airlines (MEA) is the flag carrier of Lebanon, headquartered in Beirut.

mcqs.q: SATA Air Açores is a regional airline from which country?
mcqs.explanation: SATA Air Açores is the regional airline of Portugal's Azores Islands, headquartered in Ponta Delgada.

mcqs.q: Aeroflot is the flag carrier of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Aeroflot Russian Airlines, founded in 1923, is the flag carrier of Russia — one of the world's oldest airlines.

mcqs.q: ITA Airways is the successor to which historic Italian carrier?
mcqs.explanation: ITA Airways replaced Alitalia as Italy's flag carrier in October 2021 after Alitalia's liquidation.

mcqs.q: Air France is the flag carrier of which nation?
mcqs.explanation: Air France, founded in 1933, is the flag carrier of France and part of the Air France-KLM group.

mcqs.q: SriLankan Airlines was formerly known as:
mcqs.explanation: SriLankan Airlines was known as Air Lanka from 1979 until it was rebranded SriLankan Airlines in 1998.

mcqs.q: British Airways is headquartered at which airport?
mcqs.explanation: British Airways is headquartered at the Waterside complex near London Heathrow Airport — its main hub.

mcqs.q: Cathay Pacific is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Cathay Pacific Airways, founded in 1946, is the flag carrier of Hong Kong.

mcqs.q: Emirates Airlines is based in which city?
mcqs.explanation: Emirates, founded in 1985, is based in Dubai, UAE — operator of the world's largest A380 fleet.

mcqs.q: Gulf Air is the national carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Gulf Air is the national flag carrier of the Kingdom of Bahrain, headquartered in Muharraq.

mcqs.q: Japan Airlines (JAL) is based in:
mcqs.explanation: Japan Airlines (JAL), Japan's flag carrier, is headquartered in Tokyo with its main hub at Haneda Airport.

mcqs.q: Lufthansa is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Lufthansa is the flag carrier of Germany, headquartered in Cologne with its main hubs at Frankfurt and Munich.

mcqs.q: Oman Air is the national airline of:
mcqs.explanation: Oman Air, founded in 1993, is the national flag carrier of the Sultanate of Oman, based in Muscat.

mcqs.q: Which airline was voted World's Best Airline by Skytrax in 2024?
mcqs.explanation: Qatar Airways was voted the World's Best Airline by Skytrax in 2024 — a title it has won a record number of times.

mcqs.q: Singapore Airlines is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Singapore Airlines, founded in 1947, is the flag carrier of Singapore — frequently ranked among the world's top airlines.

mcqs.q: South African Airways is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: South African Airways (SAA), founded in 1934, is the flag carrier of South Africa, based in Johannesburg.

mcqs.q: What was Pakistan's first private airline?
mcqs.explanation: Orient Airways, founded by M. A. Ispahani in 1946, was Pakistan's first private airline. It later merged with the government's Pakistan Airways in 1955 to form PIA.

mcqs.q: Nepal Airlines was formerly known as:
mcqs.explanation: Nepal Airlines was known as Royal Nepal Airlines Corporation (RNAC) until 2006, when Nepal abolished its monarchy.

mcqs.q: SAS is the joint flag airline of which three countries?
mcqs.explanation: Scandinavian Airlines System (SAS), founded in 1946, is the joint flag carrier of Norway, Sweden and Denmark.

mcqs.q: AirAsia is a low-cost airline based in:
mcqs.explanation: AirAsia is a Malaysian low-cost airline based in Kuala Lumpur — Asia's largest low-cost carrier.

mcqs.q: Air Europa is the second-largest airline of:
mcqs.explanation: Air Europa, founded in 1986, is Spain's second-largest airline after Iberia.

mcqs.q: PIA (Pakistan International Airlines) was the first Asian airline to:
mcqs.explanation: PIA was the first Asian airline to operate a jet aircraft (Boeing 707-321) in 1960. It was also the first non-communist airline to fly to China.

mcqs.q: Thai Airways International is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Thai Airways International is the flag carrier of Thailand, based in Bangkok.

mcqs.q: LOT Polish Airlines is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: LOT Polish Airlines, founded in 1929, is the flag carrier of Poland and one of the world's oldest airlines still operating.

mcqs.q: TAROM is the national airline of:
mcqs.explanation: TAROM, founded in 1954, is the flag carrier of Romania, based in Bucharest.

mcqs.q: Avianca is the second-oldest airline in the world. It is based in:
mcqs.explanation: Avianca, founded in 1919, is the flag carrier of Colombia and the second-oldest airline in the world (after KLM, also 1919).

mcqs.q: Copa Airlines is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Copa Airlines, founded in 1947, is the flag carrier of Panama, based at Tocumen International Airport.

mcqs.q: Iberia is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Iberia, founded in 1927, is the flag carrier of Spain, headquartered in Madrid.

mcqs.q: Etihad Airways is based in:
mcqs.explanation: Etihad Airways, founded in 2003, is based in Abu Dhabi — the second flag carrier of the UAE alongside Emirates.

mcqs.q: Asiana Airlines is a major carrier from:
mcqs.explanation: Asiana Airlines, founded in 1988, is one of South Korea's two major airlines (alongside Korean Air), based in Seoul.

mcqs.q: LATAM Airlines Group is the largest airline in:
mcqs.explanation: LATAM Airlines Group is the largest airline in Latin America, formed by the 2012 merger of LAN (Chile) and TAM (Brazil).

mcqs.q: Ryanair is a low-cost carrier from:
mcqs.explanation: Ryanair, founded in 1984, is an Irish low-cost airline — Europe's largest LCC by passengers carried.

mcqs.q: Mahan Air is a private airline from:
mcqs.explanation: Mahan Air, founded in 1991, is Iran's first privately owned airline, based in Tehran.

mcqs.q: Airblue is a private airline based in:
mcqs.explanation: Airblue, founded in 2003, is a Pakistani private low-cost airline based in Islamabad/Karachi.

mcqs.q: Garuda Indonesia is named after:
mcqs.explanation: Garuda Indonesia is named after Garuda — the mythical bird from Hindu/Buddhist mythology that serves as Vishnu's mount and Indonesia's national emblem.

mcqs.q: JetBlue is a low-cost carrier based in:
mcqs.explanation: JetBlue Airways, founded in 1998, is a major American low-cost carrier headquartered in New York City.

mcqs.q: Spirit Airlines is known as an:
mcqs.explanation: Spirit Airlines, headquartered in Florida, is the largest ultra-low-cost carrier (ULCC) in North America.

mcqs.q: Frontier Airlines is an eco-friendly carrier from:
mcqs.explanation: Frontier Airlines is a US ultra-low-cost carrier based in Denver, known for marketing itself as 'America's Greenest Airline'.

mcqs.q: Allegiant Air focuses on:
mcqs.explanation: Allegiant Air is a US leisure airline based in Las Vegas, focused on flying from small cities to leisure destinations.

mcqs.q: United Airlines is one of the 'Big Three' carriers in:
mcqs.explanation: The 'Big Three' US carriers are American Airlines, Delta Air Lines and United Airlines — all global majors.

mcqs.q: Southwest Airlines is the largest low-cost carrier in:
mcqs.explanation: Southwest Airlines, founded in 1967, is the largest low-cost carrier in North America by passengers carried.

mcqs.q: Pegasus Airlines is a budget carrier from:
mcqs.explanation: Pegasus Airlines, founded in 1990, is a Turkish low-cost carrier based in Istanbul.

mcqs.q: Vueling is a low-cost airline from:
mcqs.explanation: Vueling, founded in 2004, is a Spanish low-cost airline based in Barcelona — part of the IAG group.

mcqs.q: Wizz Air is a fast-growing low-cost airline from:
mcqs.explanation: Wizz Air, founded in 2003, is a Hungarian low-cost carrier headquartered in Budapest — one of Europe's fastest-growing airlines.

mcqs.q: IndiGo is the largest airline by market share in:
mcqs.explanation: IndiGo is India's largest airline by domestic market share — more than 60% of the Indian market.

mcqs.q: Vistara is a full-service airline based in:
mcqs.explanation: Vistara is an Indian full-service carrier (Tata-SIA JV) based in Gurugram. It is merging with Air India under the Tata Group.

mcqs.q: Lion Air is the largest private airline in:
mcqs.explanation: Lion Air, founded in 1999, is the largest private airline in Indonesia, headquartered in Jakarta.

mcqs.q: Scoot is a low-cost subsidiary of:
mcqs.explanation: Scoot is the low-cost subsidiary of Singapore Airlines, launched in 2012 for medium- and long-haul budget routes.

mcqs.q: Flydubai is a medium-haul carrier from:
mcqs.explanation: Flydubai, founded in 2008, is a UAE low-cost airline based in Dubai — a sister carrier to Emirates.

mcqs.q: Saudia is the national airline of:
mcqs.explanation: Saudia (formerly Saudi Arabian Airlines), founded in 1945, is the flag carrier of Saudi Arabia, based in Jeddah.

mcqs.q: Air Arabia is a budget airline based in:
mcqs.explanation: Air Arabia, founded in 2003, is a UAE low-cost airline based in Sharjah — the Middle East's first LCC.

mcqs.q: Flynas is a private low-cost airline from:
mcqs.explanation: Flynas, founded in 2007, is a Saudi private low-cost airline based in Riyadh.

mcqs.q: Gol Linhas Aéreas is a major airline in:
mcqs.explanation: Gol Linhas Aéreas, founded in 2000, is one of the largest airlines in Brazil — pioneered LCC operations in Latin America.

mcqs.q: Azul Airlines is a leading carrier in:
mcqs.explanation: Azul Brazilian Airlines, founded in 2008 by JetBlue founder David Neeleman, is one of Brazil's three major airlines.

mcqs.q: TAP Air Portugal is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: TAP Air Portugal, founded in 1945, is the flag carrier of Portugal, based in Lisbon.

mcqs.q: Finnair is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Finnair, founded in 1923, is the flag carrier of Finland — one of the oldest operating airlines in the world.

mcqs.q: SWISS (Swiss International Air Lines) is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: SWISS, founded in 2002, is the flag carrier of Switzerland, replacing the bankrupt Swissair — now part of the Lufthansa Group.

mcqs.q: Royal Jordanian is the national airline of:
mcqs.explanation: Royal Jordanian, founded in 1963, is the flag carrier of Jordan, based at Queen Alia International Airport, Amman.

mcqs.q: Royal Air Maroc is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Royal Air Maroc (RAM), founded in 1957, is the flag carrier of Morocco, based in Casablanca.

mcqs.q: EgyptAir is the national airline of:
mcqs.explanation: EgyptAir, founded in 1932, is the flag carrier of Egypt — Africa's first airline and the world's 7th-oldest airline.

mcqs.q: Ethiopian Airlines is the largest airline in:
mcqs.explanation: Ethiopian Airlines, founded in 1945 and based in Addis Ababa, is the largest airline in Africa by revenue and passengers.

mcqs.q: Turkish Airlines is known for serving the most:
mcqs.explanation: Turkish Airlines flies to more countries than any other airline in the world — over 120 nations from its Istanbul hub.

mcqs.q: China Southern Airlines is Asia's largest airline by:
mcqs.explanation: China Southern Airlines, based in Guangzhou, is Asia's largest airline by fleet size and one of the largest in the world.

mcqs.q: Vietnam Airlines is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Vietnam Airlines, founded in 1956, is the flag carrier of Vietnam, based in Hanoi.

mcqs.q: Philippine Airlines is the oldest airline in:
mcqs.explanation: Philippine Airlines (PAL), founded on 26 February 1941, is the oldest commercial airline in Asia still operating under its original name.

mcqs.q: Malaysia Airlines is the flag carrier of:
mcqs.explanation: Malaysia Airlines, descended from Malayan Airways (1947), is the flag carrier of Malaysia, based in Kuala Lumpur.

mcqs.q: Bangkok Airways brands itself as:
mcqs.explanation: Bangkok Airways markets itself as 'Asia's Boutique Airline' — Thailand's leading regional carrier with a focus on premium service.

mcqs.q: Aegean Airlines is the largest airline of:
mcqs.explanation: Aegean Airlines, founded in 1987, is the largest airline of Greece — Star Alliance member based in Athens.

mcqs.q: Riyadh Air is a new flag carrier from:
mcqs.explanation: Riyadh Air, announced in 2023 by Saudi Arabia's PIF, is a new Saudi flag carrier based in Riyadh — set to begin operations by 2025.

mcqs.q: Aeroflot is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Aeroflot is the flag carrier of Russia — one of the world's oldest airlines (founded 1923).

mcqs.q: Air Canada is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Air Canada, founded in 1937, is the flag carrier of Canada — the country's largest airline.

mcqs.q: Air France is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Air France is the flag carrier of France, headquartered at Paris-Charles de Gaulle Airport.

mcqs.q: Air India is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Air India is the flag carrier of India — privatised in 2022 to the Tata Group, which originally founded it.

mcqs.q: Airblue is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Airblue, founded in 2003, is a private Pakistani airline based in Islamabad/Karachi.

mcqs.q: Alitalia (now ITA Airways) was the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Alitalia was the historic flag carrier of Italy until 2021 — replaced by ITA Airways.

mcqs.q: Biman is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Biman Bangladesh Airlines, founded in 1972, is the flag carrier of Bangladesh.

mcqs.q: British Airways is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: British Airways, founded in 1974, is the flag carrier of the United Kingdom, headquartered at London Heathrow.

mcqs.q: Cathay Pacific is the airline of:
mcqs.explanation: Cathay Pacific is the flag carrier of Hong Kong, founded in 1946.

mcqs.q: Emirates is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Emirates is one of the two flag carriers of the UAE, based in Dubai (the other being Etihad in Abu Dhabi).

mcqs.q: Garuda is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Garuda Indonesia is the flag carrier of Indonesia, named after the mythical bird Garuda.

mcqs.q: KLM is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: KLM Royal Dutch Airlines (1919) is the flag carrier of the Netherlands — the world's oldest airline under its original name.

mcqs.q: Lufthansa is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Lufthansa is the flag carrier of Germany, founded in 1953 (re-established post-war).

mcqs.q: PIA (Pakistan International Airlines) is the airline of:
mcqs.explanation: PIA, founded in 1955, is the flag carrier of Pakistan — and the first Asian airline to fly a jet aircraft.

mcqs.q: Qantas is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Qantas, founded in 1920, is the flag carrier of Australia — the world's oldest continuously operating airline.

mcqs.q: Shaheen Airlines was the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Shaheen Air International was a Pakistani private airline (1993–2018), based in Karachi.

mcqs.q: Brussels Airlines is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: Brussels Airlines is the flag carrier of Belgium, based in Brussels — part of the Lufthansa Group.

mcqs.q: Singapore Airlines is the airline of:
mcqs.explanation: Singapore Airlines is the flag carrier of Singapore — frequently ranked as one of the world's best airlines.

mcqs.q: Swiss Airlines (SWISS) is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: SWISS International Air Lines is the flag carrier of Switzerland — successor to Swissair.

mcqs.q: American Airlines is the airline of which country?
mcqs.explanation: American Airlines is the world's largest airline by fleet and passengers — headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas, USA.
